    <h1 {...{
      "id": "brand-concierge-implementation-guide-ios"
    }}>{`Brand Concierge Implementation Guide (iOS)`}</h1>
    <p>{`The Brand Concierge UI is presented in two steps:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Enable UI presentation`}</strong>{` by wrapping your SwiftUI root with `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`Concierge.wrap(...)`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Open the chat`}</strong>{` by calling `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`Concierge.show(...)`}</inlineCode></li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`Internally, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Concierge.show(...)`}</inlineCode>{` dispatches an event in the Adobe Experience Platform Mobile SDK that the Concierge extension handles to build a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ConciergeConfiguration`}</inlineCode>{`, then the SwiftUI overlay presents `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ChatView`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <hr></hr>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "prerequisites"
    }}>{`Prerequisites`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "required-sdk-modules"
    }}>{`Required SDK modules`}</h3>
    <p>{`Your app needs the following Experience Platform SDKs to be available and registered:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`AEPCore`}</strong>{` (MobileCore; Configuration shared state from `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`configureWith(appId:)`}</inlineCode>{`)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`AEPEdge`}</strong></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`AEPEdgeIdentity`}</strong></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`AEPBrandConcierge`}</strong></li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "ios-version"
    }}>{`iOS version`}</h3>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Minimum iOS 15.0+`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "permissions-for-speech-to-text-optional"
    }}>{`Permissions for speech to text (optional)`}</h3>
    <p>{`Speech to text uses iOS Speech and microphone APIs. Add these to your app `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Info.plist`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`NSMicrophoneUsageDescription`}</inlineCode></strong></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription`}</inlineCode></strong></li>
    </ul>
    <hr></hr>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "configuration"
    }}>{`Configuration`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "step-1-set-up-the-adobe-experience-platform-mobile-sdk"
    }}>{`Step 1: Set up the Adobe Experience Platform Mobile SDK`}</h3>
    <p>{`Follow the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "/aep-mobile-sdkdocs/src/pages/home/getting-started/"
      }}>{`Adobe Experience Platform Mobile SDK getting started guide`}</a>{` to set up the base SDK integration used by Concierge.`}</p>
    <p>{`The required extensions are:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`AEPCore`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`AEPEdge`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`AEPEdgeIdentity`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`AEPBrandConcierge`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "step-2-validate-the-brand-concierge-configuration-keys"
    }}>{`Step 2: Validate the Brand Concierge configuration keys`}</h3>
    <p>{`If you set the Adobe Experience Platform SDK log level to trace:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`MobileCore.setLogLevel(.trace)
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`you can then inspect the app logs to confirm that extension shared states are being set with the expected values.`}</p>
    <p>{`Brand Concierge expects the following keys in the Configuration shared state:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`concierge.server`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: String (server host or base domain for Concierge requests)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`concierge.configId`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: String (datastream ID)`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`The ECID is read from the Edge Identity shared state.`}</p>
    <p>{`Another option for validation is to use Adobe Assurance. Refer to the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "/aep-mobile-sdkdocs/src/pages/home/getting-started/validate/"
      }}>{`Mobile SDK validation guide`}</a>{` for more information.`}</p>
    <hr></hr>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "optional-styling"
    }}>{`Optional styling`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "theme-injection-recommended"
    }}>{`Theme injection (recommended)`}</h3>
    <p>{`The Brand Concierge chat interface can be customized by loading a theme JSON and applying it above `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Concierge.wrap(...)`}</inlineCode>{` so both the floating button and the overlay use it. The UI reads styling from the SwiftUI environment value `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`conciergeTheme`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`let theme = ConciergeThemeLoader.load(from: "theme-default", in: .main) ?? ConciergeThemeLoader.default()

var body: some View {
    Concierge.wrap(AppRootView(), surfaces: ["my-surface"], hideButton: true)
        .conciergeTheme(theme)
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`More information regarding theme customization can be found in the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "/aep-mobile-sdkdocs/edge/adobe-brand-concierge/ios/style-guide/"
      }}>{`Style guide (iOS)`}</a>{`.`}</p>
    <hr></hr>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "basic-usage"
    }}>{`Basic usage`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "option-a--manual-api-call-no-floating-button"
    }}>{`Option A — Manual API call (no floating button)`}</h3>
    <p>{`Use this when you want full control over where the entry point lives.`}</p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Wrap your root content and hide the built-in button:`}</li>
    </ol>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`Concierge.wrap(AppRootView(), surfaces: ["my-surface"], hideButton: true)
`}</code></pre>
    <ol {...{
      "start": 2
    }}>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Trigger chat from your own UI:`}</li>
    </ol>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`Button("Chat") {
    Concierge.show(surfaces: ["my-surface"], title: "Concierge", subtitle: "Powered by Adobe")
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Optional: provide `}<strong parentName="p">{`speech capture`}</strong>{` (default is created internally if not passed) and `}<strong parentName="p">{`text to speech`}</strong>{` by passing a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`TextSpeaking`}</inlineCode>{` implementation (text to speech is off by default unless you supply one).`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "option-b--floating-button-built-in"
    }}>{`Option B — Floating button (built-in)`}</h3>
    <p>{`Use this for a drop-in entry point:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`Concierge.wrap(AppRootView(), surfaces: ["my-surface"]) // hideButton defaults to false
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`This shows a floating button in the bottom trailing corner; tapping it calls `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Concierge.show(surfaces:)`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "closing-the-ui"
    }}>{`Closing the UI`}</h3>
    <p>{`Dismiss the overlay from code with:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`Concierge.hide()
`}</code></pre>
    <hr></hr>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "uikit-usage"
    }}>{`UIKit usage`}</h2>
    <p>{`Use this when your app is UIKit-based and you want to present Concierge from a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`UIViewController`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "present-the-chat-ui"
    }}>{`Present the chat UI`}</h3>
    <p>{`Call `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Concierge.present(on:surfaces:title:subtitle:)`}</inlineCode>{` from the view controller that should host the chat:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`import AEPBrandConcierge

final class MyViewController: UIViewController {
    @objc private func openChat() {
        Concierge.present(on: self, surfaces: ["my-surface"], title: "Concierge", subtitle: "Powered by Adobe")
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "dismiss-the-chat-ui"
    }}>{`Dismiss the chat UI`}</h3>
    <p>{`To dismiss the presented UI:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`Concierge.hide()
`}</code></pre>
    <hr></hr>
